figures.caption: Figure 1 This figure illustrates the result of Theorem 4.2. It can be viewed as an automaton over the alphabet {U, F, F̂, X, ∧, ∨, ¬, ⊤, ⊥} that accepts exactly those words that satisfy the following conditions: (1) they include at least one temporal operator; (2) occurrences of operators respect the order induced by the alphabet; and (3) the set of operators is not a superset of any of the negative fragments listed in Theorem B.2. The leaves of the figure correspond to negative fragments (shown in red), each annotated with the corresponding item of Theorem B.2 (or to nodes from which no further operators can be read), whereas inner nodes correspond to positive fragments (shown in green). It follows that the accepted words correspond to sets of operators that are subsets of the positive fragments listed in Theorem B.1. To reduce clutter, some negative fragments appear more than once.
